name: Weak cryptographic hash (MD5/SHA-1)
description: hashlib.md5() or hashlib.sha1() is used for hashing. MD5 and SHA-1 are cryptographically broken. Practical collision attacks exist, making these algorithms unsuitable for integrity verification, digital signatures, or password hashing.
remediation: Replace hashlib.md5() and hashlib.sha1() with hashlib.sha256() or hashlib.sha3_256() for integrity checks. For password hashing, use bcrypt, scrypt, or argon2-cffi. In FIPS environments, ensure the chosen algorithm is FIPS-approved.

patterns.message_template: hashlib.md5() uses a broken hash algorithm
hashlib.sha1() uses a broken hash algorithm
